data.title: First Hawaiian GAAP EPS of $0.58 beats by $0.09, revenue of $217.54M beats by $2.66M
data.description:
• First Hawaiian press release (NASDAQ:FHB): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.58 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $217.54M (+6.3% Y/Y) beats by $2.66M.
  • Total loans and leases increased $58.8 million versus prior quarter.
  • Total deposits increased $15.6 million versus prior quarter.
  • Net interest margin increased 3 basis points to 3.11%.
  • Recorded a $4.5 million provision for credit losses.
  • Provision for income taxes included a net benefit of $5.1 million due a recent change in the California tax code.
  • Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.26 per share.

  • Total assets were $23.8 billion at June 30, 2025 versus $23.7 billion at March 31, 2025.

  • Gross loans and leases were $14.4 billion as of June 30, 2025, an increase of $58.8 million, or 0.4%, from $14.3 billion as of March 31, 2025.

  • Total deposits were $20.2 billion as of June 30, 2025, an increase of $15.6 million from March 31, 2025.

  • During the quarter ended June 30, 2025, we recorded a $4.5 million provision for credit losses. In the quarter ended March 31, 2025, we recorded a $10.5 million provision for credit losses.

data.title: Gentex Non-GAAP EPS of $0.47 beats by $0.08, revenue of $657.9M beats by $36.72M
data.description:
• Gentex press release (NASDAQ:GNTX): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.47 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $657.9M (+14.8% Y/Y) beats by $36.72M.
  • Gentex and VOXX Consolidated net sales totaled $657.9 million, a 15% increase compared to the second quarter of 2024, which did not include VOXX

  • 2025 Annual Guidance (as of July 25, 2025)

    • Consolidated Revenue: $2.44 – $2.61 billion vs $2.46B (New consolidated guidance, previously: $2.15 – $2.32 billion)
      • Gentex primary markets: $2.10 – $2.20 billion
      • Gentex China market: $100 – $125 million
      • VOXX Revenue estimate: $240 – $280 million
    • Gross Margin: 33% – 34% (New consolidated guidance)
      • Gentex (stand-alone): 34% – 34.5% (Previously 33% - 34%)
      • VOXX (stand-alone): 27% – 29%
    • Operating Expenses (New consolidated guidance, excluding severance): $370 – $390 million
      • Gentex: $300 – $310 million (unchanged)
      • VOXX: $70 – $80 million
    • Tax Rate: 16% – 17% (previously: 15% – 17%)
    • Capital Expenditures: $100 – $125 million (unchanged)
    • Depreciation & Amortization: $91 – $98 million (New consolidated guidance)
      • Gentex: $90 – $95 million
      • VOXX: $1 - $3 million

data.title: Customers Bancorp Chairman and CEO Jay Sidhu to become executive chairman
data.description:
Customers Bancorp (NYSE:CUBI) announced on Friday the transition of Chairman and CEO Jay Sidhu to the role of executive chairman, effective January 1, 2026.

Sam Sidhu, retaining his title as president of the bank holding company, will succeed Jay Sidhu as CEO.

data.title: Flagstar cuts 2025 guidance after Q2 results miss as restructuring continues.
data.description:
Flagstar Financial (NYSE:FLG) lowered its guidance for 2025 net interest income and earnings, as the company continues to restructure after weathering a liquidity scare in 2024. It reaffirmed earnings guidance for 2026 and 2027.

Q2 earnings showed progress in restructuring its balance sheet and cutting costs. After the close on Thursday, Flagstar (NYSE:FLG) said it plans to merge the company into its bank in order to simplify its structure and reduce costs.

The company that restructured with $1B in new capital in 2024 from a group led by former Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin now expects full-year 2025 adjusted loss per share of $0.35-$0.40, wider than its previous guidance of a loss per share of $0.25-$0.30.

Guidance for full-year 2025 net interest income was cut to $1.70B-$1.75B from $1.825B-$1.875B. The new guidance is softer than the Visible Alpha consensus of $1.83B.

The company still expects 2026 adjusted EPS of $0.75-$0.80 and 2027 adjusted EPS of $2.10-$2.20.

Q2 adjusted EPS of -$0.14, just missing the average analyst estimate of -$0.13, narrowed from -$0.23 in Q1 and -$1.05 in last year's Q2.

"We made further gains on our C&I and Private Bank growth strategy, our credit quality metrics continue to improve, we continued to lower our operating expenses, reduced our commercial real estate exposure, and we also increased our net interest margin," said Chairman, President and CEO Joseph M. Otting.

Flagstar (FLG) stock rose 0.8% in premarket trading.

Total revenue of $496M, missing the $518M consensus, increased from $490M in the previous quarter and declined from $671M a year ago.

Net interest income of $419M, lagging the Visible Alpha estimate of $436M, climbed from $410M in Q1 and dropped from $557M in Q2 2024.

Q2 adjusted preprovision net revenue improved to $9M from -$23M in the prior quarter and nil in the year-ago quarter.

Total adjusted noninterest income declined to $77M in Q2 from $80M in Q1 and $114M in last year's Q2.

Provision for credit losses of $64M, vs. the Visible Alpha estimate of $66M, was down from $79M in the prior quarter and $390M in the year-ago period.

Adjusted operating expenses of $460M fell from $485M in Q1 and $638M in last year's Q2.

Total loans and leases held for investment fell to $64.1B at June 30, 2025, from $66.6B at March 31. Deposits of $69.7B dipped from $73.9B at the end of Q1.

Conference call at 8:00 AM ET.

data.title: Bristol Myers Squibb taps Cristian Massacesi as CMO
data.description:
Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE:BMY) has tapped Cristian Massacesi, as executive vice president, chief medical officer, and head of development, effective August 1, 2025.

Massacesi most recently served as chief medical officer of AstraZeneca and Alexion and as oncology chief development officer at AstraZeneca. In the new role, Massacesi will oversee the company’s early-stage and late-stage product development across all therapeutic areas.

Samit Hirawat, Bristol Myers Squibb’s current executive vice president, chief medical officer, and head of development, is stepping down to pursue new professional opportunities. Hirawat will transition from his current role, effective August 1, 2025, and will remain an advisor to the company through November 1, 2025.

data.title: Pfizer, BioNTech win EU backing for updated COVID shot
data.description:
• An expert panel of the EU drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency (EMA) on Friday recommended marketing authorization for Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) and BioNTech’s (NASDAQ:BNTX) COVID-19 shot, updated to target the LP.8.1 variant of the virus.
  • The positive opinion from the EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) allows the companies to market the monovalent shot for COVID prevention in those aged six months and older in Europe.
  • The duo expects to immediately ship the retooled vaccine in all applicable countries, subject to a final decision from the European Commission on the marketing authorization, which is expected “soon,” Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) and BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX) said.
  • The companies added that manufacturing of the LP.8.1-adjusted shot has already begun to ensure there are enough supplies during the upcoming fall and winter season.

data.title: Veracyte to replace Triumph in S&P SmallCap 600 index
data.description:
Biotechnology company Veracyte (NASDAQ:VCYT) is set to replace Triumph Group (NYSE:TGI) in the S&P SmallCap 600 index prior to the market open on Tuesday, July 29.

VCYT shares were +6.49% Friday pre-market to $25.12.

The news comes in the wake of Triumph's acquisition by private equity firms Warburg Pincus and Berkshire Partners for an enterprise value of ~$3B. The deal is expected to close soon.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Phillips 66 surpasses Q2 estimates; initiates Q3 outlook
C2 Blockchain boosts DOG Coin reserve
data.description:
• C2 Blockchain (OTCPK:CBLO) has acquired an additional 61.5M DOG Coins at an average price of $0.0038 per coin, bringing the company's total holdings to around 133.29M DOG Coins.
  • “This milestone solidifies C2 Blockchain's position as the largest known holder of DOG Coin among publicly listed companies,” the company said.
